publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-09-11 18:16:45 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Diaries, Manuscripts, Photographs, Printed Materials, Publications, Reports, Research Materials
did.abstract: Alphabetical correspondence, files relating to European trips, manuscripts, slides, and photographs relating to his research on siting vineyards, grapevine physiology, vineyard mechanization and management, mineral nutrition, rootstocks, and canopy microclimates. Also research pertaining to an unfinished book.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Evan Fay Earle, September 2007, August 2014, November 2014

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Viticulture, New York State Agricultural Experiment Station, Geneva.

Nelson Shaulis graduated with a B.S. in horticulture and an M.S. in agronomy from Pennsylvania State University. He received his Ph.D. from Cornell in 1941. He served as a soil conservationist with the USDA Soil Conservation Service from 1938 to 1944, while he was also an instructor and assistant professor of pomology at Penn State. In 1944, he was appointed assistant professor of pomology at Cornell, and became professor of pomology and viticulture from 1948 to 1967. He retired as professor of viticulture in 1978. His major contributions to the grape industry included a training system for grapes called the Geneva Double Curtain (GDC), which he initiated at the Experiment Station in 1960 and with growers in 1964. He also helped to develop the mechanical grape harvester. Shaulis was named a fellow of the American Society of Horticultural Science in 1972 and in 1997 was the recipient of the Merit Award given by the American Society for Enology and Viticulture (ASEV), the highest award of the society. He also received Merit Awards from the Society of Wine Educators, the American Wine Society, the New York State Wine and Grape Foundation, and the National Grape Cooperative.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Alphabetical correspondence, files relating to European trips, manuscripts, slides, and photographs relating to his research on siting vineyards, grapevine physiology, vineyard mechanization and management, mineral nutrition, rootstocks, and canopy microclimates. Also research pertaining to an unfinished book.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Forms part of the Eastern Wine and Grape Archive. (EWGA)
Related material located in collection #8656, Chisholm-Ryder mechanical grape harvester records.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Pom and PM followed by a number indicate 5.5" x 7.75" negative envelopes with photos of plants, fields, or charts/tables. Only a very few also have slides or negatives enclosed.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Portions of unpublished book are located in Boxes 26, and in various folders throughout the collection which include "book" in the title, non-exclusively.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Colored, but blank post-it notes were attached so some boxes of slides but no key available. These notes were left attached to the first section heading card in the box.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Nelson J. Shaulis papers, #22-2-3140. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a finding aid for Boxes 1-22, also a bibliography of Shaulis' publications.

did.unittitle: Concord Grape Maturity and Flavor, Environmental Factor Study etc.

scopecontent.p: Also remakrs about the Concord grape prepared by Richard Wellington at teh request of Walter Taylor for the opening of the Taylor Museum in Hammondsport, July 3, 1967.

did.unittitle: The Relation Between Direction of Growth and Vigor of Grapes by A. M. Burroughs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grape Belt, Ohio, Mesilla Valley New Mexico, Virginia, Minnesota, New Hampshire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: U.S. and Canadian grape production data as early as 1909. 1945 Grape census. Also includes Annual Finger Lakes Grape Tour data from the 1940s, published in 1949, inludes the following vineyeards, Elvira, Granby, Beers, Fox, Kimbar (at the German prison camp), Depot, all these were owned by Widmer Wine Cellars.

did.unittitle: Geneva Double Curtain and Other Trellising Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of Cellar Notes and an article on Wine Growing in the Finger Lakes from the American Wine Society Journal.

did.unittitle: Publications on climate and vineyard growth in developing wine areas
Miscellaneous Grape Research Publications and preprints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes paper on Promising New Grape Selections form the New York State Breeding Program with descriptions of many crosses. Also papers oninterstock and vigor grant proposal, heat treated riesling, Ethrel trials at Taylor vineyard, Alar, American Journal of Enology and Viticulture Guide to Authors. Also a bilbiography of works, possibly used in class Horticulture 355.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes, International Symposium on Cool Climate Viticulture and Enology at Wine Expo '84, NEC-9 minutes.

did.unittitle: Material on Grape Growing in Virginia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sabbatic report and report to Michigan Viticultural Advisory Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to John Einset from J. A. Savignac of Maison Champagneur in Joliette, Quebec Canada about their vineyard varieties.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Mark Kliewer about trellis systems in cold climates. Also information regarding terminology for grapevine canopies and a paper titled Some Observations on Terminology Useful in Describing Grapevines. Also scans of some of these items on a disc.
